content.full_text:
August 21, 1990

Ms. Lynda Cassady  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
Sacramento, CA 95814-0814

Re: Use Of Private Airplane By Candidate

Dear Lynda:

As you know, we spoke on July 13, 1990, and again on August 21, 1990, regarding the use by a candidate of a private airplane for campaign-related purposes. This letter is intended to summarize those two telephone conversations.

On July 13, 1990, you confirmed to me that the Peter Stone opinion (3 FPPC Opinions 52, June 9, 1977) establishes that in the case of free air transportation, the fair market value of the travel must be determined. In this regard, the filer can use the commercial air rate or the charter rate divided by the number of passengers in estimating the value of the flight. You advised me that the Stone opinion, which deals with how to disclose gifts on a Statement of Economic Interests, is also considered by your office to be applicable to campaign disclosure. You further clarified that the cost of the airplane can be apportioned between the campaign and non-campaign passengers that are taken on a particular trip.

On August 21, 1990, you clarified further how to apply the commercial or charter rates to particular flights. You said that a convenience factor to the candidate does not have to be built in. Basically, you said that the fair market value in using a private airplane for transportation is either the commercial rate or the charter rate for the flight. You said that if a commercial rate, established by commercial air carriers, can be determined for a flight between two particular points, then that commercial rate can be used. If there is no established commercial rate available, than the charter rate must be used. The charter rate can also be used alternatively even if a commercial rate is ascertainable. You indicated that the actual costs for operating the private aircraft is not available as a third option for determining value, since the Stone opinion clearly establishes that either the commercial rate or the charter rate must be used in determining fair market value.

I assume that the commercial rate that can be used would be

Ms. Lynda Cassady  
August 21, 1990  
Page Two

the normal and customary airfare charged by a particular airline to the public generally. This could include a standard discounted fare that would be available to the public generally during the period in which the air transportation occurs.

Thank you for your advice and cooperation.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Peter A. Bagatilos

PAB/jjm

feinstei:8-21lc

TO: Wayne Imberie and Jonathon Rothman  
FROM: Trudy Washburn  
RE: July 27th and July 30th conversation

I spoke with Wayne on July 27th and with Jonathon on July 30th.

I would like confirmation of my understanding of the following procedures that I must do in order to comply with the Fair Campaign Practices rules.

1. I must file a notice of termination of our 1988 campaign (form 415).

2. A Statement of Organization (recipient committee) form 410 must be filed for the 1990 campaign.

3. Wayne informed me that since we have never used a form 520 on our old campaign bank account I can now file a form 520 using the funds and the same bank account for the 1990 campaign. This was confirmed by Jonathon on July 30th. I would like to mail form 520 today. Please confirm.

If this is not correct please advise by FAX by 5:00 p.m. today, August 6, 1990, as we intend to take steps to implement the above understanding.

Our FAX number is (415) 644-6035.

Thank you.
